insight: Lancetooth is an absorb-denying unit: its cost is poor under normal circumstances, but when the opponent would simply absorb the attack, the 2 damage becomes effectively free, making Lancetooth a strong purchase specifically when it denies absorb.
author: 307th
context: Discussion of a specific set featuring Lancetooth, Wild Drone, and Borehole Patroller, analyzing when Lancetooth is worth buying.

insight: Wild Drone's main downside—that opponents can kill your Wild Drones to deny absorb—is negated when Lancetooth is in the set, because the opponent can deny absorb via Lancetooth anyway even if you skip Wild Drones. This makes going 2-4 Wild Drones strongly correct in such sets.
author: 307th
context: Set analysis where Lancetooth and Wild Drone are both available, evaluating how their interaction affects the decision to buy Wild Drones.

insight: Borehole Patroller works especially well alongside Lancetooth and Wild Drone because its 2-health body can serve as an efficient absorber, and the absorb-denial pressure from Lancetooth and Wild Drone synergizes with Borehole's defensive utility.
author: 307th
context: Set review where 307th and mqp discuss optimal strategy involving Borehole Patroller, Lancetooth, and Wild Drone.

insight: In a set with Wild Drone, the recommended opening for Player 1 is: turn 1 Wild Drone, turn 2 Wild Drone + Conduit, turn 3 Wild Drone + Blastforge, into Borehole Patroller. This line takes early priority over other infrastructure.
author: 307th
context: 307th and mqp discussing Player 1 optimal opening in a set with Wild Drone and Borehole Patroller.

insight: In Wild Drone sets, it is generally recommended to spend the first two turns buying Wild Drones before branching into other tech, as Wild Drone provides immediate and compounding economic value that outweighs delaying it.
author: 307th
context: General strategic advice on Wild Drone openings discussed during a set analysis.

insight: Draws in Prismata most commonly occur when both players are breaching but neither committed to being breach-proof, which is why units like Tia Threnody and Iceblade appear at the top of draw-rate statistics.
author: namington
context: Discussion of prismata-stats.com unit win/draw rates, analyzing why certain units correlate with high draw rates.

insight: Redeemer has a notably high Player 2 win rate (~55% based on prismata-stats.com data at the time), suggesting it significantly favors the second player, likely because Player 2 can more easily time absorb and defenses against Redeemer's attack pattern.
author: f300xen
context: Community discussion of unit win-rate statistics from prismata-stats.com, noting Redeemer's unusually high P2 win rate.

insight: Antima Comet can be set up via a specific P2 econ line (DD/DDE/DDDC/DDDB/DDWA/Ant, then AuAuAu EEE, then Engineer buyout) that generates exactly enough gold to buy out all Engineers, producing a 22-Antima hit if no Engineers are lost during setup.
author: liadahlia
context: Liadahlia described and executed a specific P2 Antima Comet line that ended in an Engineer buyout for a 22-Antima hit.

insight: Apollo generally beats Vai Mauronax (Ampo) most of the time in medium-sized games, because Ampo requires substantial red support (e.g., Tarsiers) to be viable; in large games Ampo can still be worth buying to deal 9-10 damage and eat snipes, or in small games to force out Apollo before winning with other red support.
author: apooche
context: Discussion of Ampo vs Apollo matchup dynamics when both units are in the same set.

insight: Vai Mauronax (Ampo) loses to Tatsu Nullifier (Tanta) even though Tanta is a mid-economy rather than aggressive unit, suggesting Ampo is vulnerable to efficient pressure units that do not rely on pure rush strategies.
author: liadahlia
context: Discussion of which units counter Ampo, with liadahlia asserting Tanta beats it despite not being an aggro unit.

insight: Delaying activation of Kinetic Driver by one turn in a Cryo Ray vs. Arka-equipped opponent scenario can be correct: it prevents the opponent from counter-Arka-ing, forcing them into a weaker position on the critical high-damage turn (e.g., a 7-Cryo Ray attack).
author: liadahlia
context: Post-game analysis of a Cryo Ray vs. Defense Grid game where timing of Kinetic Driver activation was debated.

insight: The prismata-stats.com website tracks per-unit win rates and draw rates across games, but the data has known limitations: it does not filter by Elo, does not account for balance patches over time, and may include unequal sample sizes across units (e.g., newer units like Savior had fewer games).
author: namington
context: Community discussion evaluating the reliability of prismata-stats.com unit statistics.
